prompt:
You are Agent 9 of 12 working on a codebase at /var/folders/45/myq5db_n6q5cyhtb39vkbmp80000gn/T/twining-bench-PlNDkg.

## Codebase Orientation
This is a TypeScript service (~1,140 lines, ~24 files).

Directory structure:
```
src/
  models/        # Data models and interfaces
  services/      # Business logic layer
  repositories/  # Data access layer
  utils/         # Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
  events/        # Event definitions (if using event-driven pattern)
  adapters/      # External service adapters (email, webhook, etc.)
  validators/    # Validation logic
  config/        # Configuration
tests/           # Test files mirroring src/ structure
```

Your task: Update user notification preferences to support multiple channels (email + SMS + webhook).

Specifically:
1. Check coordination context — the previous session (Session 8) added SMS support and flagged that user preferences need updating.
2. Review the current user preferences model and service from Session 3.
3. The original preferences assumed email-only. This assumption is no longer valid — SMS was added in Session 8.
4. Refactor the preferences model to support per-channel settings (email, SMS, webhook).
5. Update the preferences service to handle multi-channel preferences.
6. Update the notification service to check per-channel preferences before sending.
7. Update tests to cover multi-channel scenarios.
8. Make sure the codebase compiles and all tests pass.

Important:
- This is a conscious decision change — the email-only assumption from Session 3 is explicitly invalidated.
- Preserve backward compatibility where possible (existing email preferences should still work).
- Document the decision change and why.

parameters.task: Update user notification preferences to support multiple channels (email + SMS + webhook). Refactor from email-only to per-channel settings.

alternatives.option: Single unified ChannelSettings interface with channel discriminator
alternatives.reason_rejected: Each channel has different frequency options (email supports daily/weekly digests, SMS/webhook only instant/never). A unified type would either lose type safety or require awkward union types.

alternatives.option: Keep email opt-out but make SMS/webhook also opt-out (enabled by default)
alternatives.reason_rejected: SMS and webhook require infrastructure (phone number, webhook URL) that users must explicitly configure. Defaulting to enabled would cause failed deliveries for all users.

assumptions: SMS and webhook are opt-in channels (disabled by default)
Email remains opt-out (enabled by default) for backward compatibility
Each channel's preferences are checked independently in dispatch()
parameters.context: Session 8 added SMS adapter support, invalidating the email-only assumption from Session 3 decision 01KN78DXZCATNERNHV29Z9AWSZ. The preferences model, service, and notification dispatch needed updating to support multi-channel preference checking.
parameters.rationale: Each channel (email, SMS, webhook) gets its own settings interface with enabled, frequency, and subscribedCategories. Email defaults to enabled (backward compatible with existing behavior). SMS and webhook default to disabled (opt-in) because they require explicit user action (providing phone number, configuring webhook URL). The notification service dispatch checks preferences per-channel rather than only for email. This design keeps channels independent — enabling SMS doesn't affect email settings.
parameters.summary: Refactor user preferences from email-only to per-channel settings supporting email, SMS, and webhook independently

parameters.detail:
Completed:
1. PREFERENCES MODEL (src/models/user-preferences.ts): Added SmsNotificationSettings, WebhookNotificationSettings interfaces with channel-appropriate frequency types (SmsFrequency: instant|never, WebhookFrequency: instant|never). Added sms and webhook fields to UserPreferences. Updated Create/Update input types. Added DEFAULT_SMS_SETTINGS and DEFAULT_WEBHOOK_SETTINGS (both disabled by default — opt-in channels).
2. PREFERENCES SERVICE (src/services/preferences.service.ts): Added shouldSendSms() and shouldSendWebhook() methods mirroring shouldSendEmail(). Updated createPreferences() and updatePreferences() to handle all three channels. SMS/webhook return false when no preferences exist (opt-in), email returns true (opt-out, backward compatible).
3. NOTIFICATION SERVICE (src/services/notification.service.ts): Updated dispatch() to check per-channel preferences — email, sms, and webhook each route to the appropriate shouldSend method.
4. PREFERENCES REPOSITORY (src/repositories/preferences.repository.ts): Added sms and webhook fields to toDomain/toRecord serialization.
5. EXPORTS (src/index.ts): Added SmsNotificationSettings, SmsFrequency, WebhookNotificationSettings, WebhookFrequency types and DEFAULT_SMS_SETTINGS, DEFAULT_WEBHOOK_SETTINGS constants.
6. TESTS (tests/services/preferences.service.test.ts): 22 new tests covering shouldSendSms, shouldSendWebhook, multi-channel create/update, and multi-channel dispatch scenarios.
7. All 216 tests pass (22 new + 194 existing). TypeScript compiles clean.
8. Decision 01KN79AS0HDBDR344B4DE1THC1 supersedes 01KN78DXZCATNERNHV29Z9AWSZ (email-only assumption invalidated).

Key design choices:
- Email: opt-out (enabled by default) — backward compatible
- SMS/Webhook: opt-in (disabled by default) — require explicit user configuration
- Each channel independently configured with enabled, frequency, subscribedCategories
parameters.summary: Agent 9 complete: Multi-channel preferences (email+SMS+webhook) with 22 new tests. All 216 tests pass.
